rulings.Case Title: MOLALLA HOLDINGS, INC., DBA vs. KANNA, MOHAMMAD M. AKA MOHAMMAD KANNA AKA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate Renewal Of Judgment And To Vacate Entry Of Default And Default Judgment For Lack Of Actual Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 31, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT MOHAMMAD KANNA Motion To Vacate Renewal Of Judgment And To Vacate Entry Of Default And Default Judgment For Lack Of Actual Notice. Motion is denied. Motion not made within a "reasonable time." The judgment in this case has been on file for over ten years. Insufficient demonstration of lack of proper service. The expiration of the business license does not show improper service. It appears the store was still operating under defendant's liquor license when service was effectuated, and escrow on the sale of the business did not close until 2000. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: COASTSIDE LUMBER SUPPLY INC A CALIFORNIA VS. MARTIN GERARD CURRAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 31, 2009, line 6, CROSS COMPLAINANT SCOTT HALL MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary adjudication is granted. No triable issue of material fact raised. Cross-complainant's objections to evidence sustained. But even if objections overruled, no triable issue of material fact. Business & Professions Code section 7031 controls without exception. No enforceable settlement occurred. In re Marriage of Assemi (1994) 7 Cal.4th 896 distinguishable because the issue in that case was whether a settlenment before a court appointed retired judge/arbitrator was "before the Court" for purposes of CCP section 664.6. The Court adopts cross-complainant's Propsed Order granting summary adjudication, except for correction of Proposed Order's title. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DIOSDADO FACTOR JR. VS. MERCY HOUSING CORP. et al

rulings.Case Title: SHARON A ALGER VS. ROSA FORNESI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 31, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT ROSA FORNESI, ENRICO FORNESI MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION to the first amended complaint filed May 15, 2009 is denied as moot. Plaintiff filed her second amended complaint on July 14, 2009. An amended complaint supercedes the original and furnishes the sole basis for the cause of action. The original complaint is dropped out of the case and ceases to have any effect as a pleading, or as a basis for a judgment. Anmaco, Inc. vs. Bohlken (1993) 13 Cal.App.4th 891, 901. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GARY E GROTE et al VS. DAVID DRIVER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Hearing On Demurrer To Second Amended Complaint And Notice Of Motion To Strike Of Defts David Driver, Emilia Rallapalli, Krishn Rallapalli And Phillpine Rallapalli
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 31, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT DAVID DRIVER, RALLAPALLI, EMELIA Amended Notice Of Hearing On Demurrer To Second Amended Complaint And Notice Of Motion To Strike Of Defendants David Driver, Emilia Rallapalli, Krishn Rallapalli And Phillpine Rallapalli. Demurrer is sustained in part, and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to Plaintiff Hussein regarding the sixth cause of action for trespass. Hussein fails to plead trespass damaging on ownership interest as described in Hassoldt vs. Patrick Media Group (2000) 84 Cal.App.4th 153, 171. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Demurrer overrruled as to remaining causes of action. Motion to strike is denied, ten days leave to answer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KUSHAM GUPTA et al VS. MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA et al

Court unaware of opposition at time of hearing on demurrer. Relief granted pursuant to CCP section 473. Court further rules on underlying demurrer: sustained with five days leave to amend. =(302/CWW)
